question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a single GET /api/v1/network/nodes request with the maximum allowed number of node.id=eq:<value> parameters (up to the list size limit accepted by nodeIdParams) to force NetworkServiceImpl to build a large Long[] nodeIdArray and pass it to findNetworkNodes() via the any(:nodeIds) SQL clause, causing PostgreSQL to perform a large IN-list scan against address_book_entry, thereby increasing DB CPU consumption by over 30% compared to baseline?
